children.text: health benefits described in subsection (b), and
in the case of an eligible beneficiary described in subsection (f)(1), death benefits coverage described in subsection (c).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The trustees of the Combined Fund shall provide health care benefits to each eligible beneficiary by enrolling the beneficiary in a health care services plan which undertakes to provide such benefits on a prepaid risk basis. The trustees shall utilize all available plan resources to ensure that, consistent with paragraph (2), coverage under the managed care system shall to the maximum extent feasible be substantially the same as (and subject to the same limitations of) coverage provided under the 1950 UMWA Benefit Plan and the 1974 UMWA Benefit Plan as of .January 1, 1992

children.label: i
children.text: vary as necessary to ensure that beneficiaries in different geographic areas have access to a uniform level of health benefits; and

children.label: ii
children.text: result in aggregate payments for such plan year from the Combined Fund which do not exceed the total premium payments required to be paid to the Combined Fund under section 9704(a) for the plan year, adjusted as provided in subparagraphs (B) and (C).

children.label: i
children.text: by the aggregate death benefit premiums determined under section 9704(c) for the plan year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: by the amount reserved for plan administration under subsection (d).

children.label: i
children.text: by any reduction in the total premium payments required to be paid under section 9704(a) by reason of transfers described in section 9705,

children.label: I
children.text: is derived from amounts described in section 9704(e)(3)(B)(i), and

children.label: II
children.text: the trustees elect to use to pay benefits for the current plan year, and

children.label: iii
children.text: any interest earned by the Combined Fund which the trustees elect to use to pay benefits for the current plan year.

children.heading: Qualified providers
children.label: 3 Qualified providers
children.text: The trustees of the Combined Fund shall not enter into an agreement under paragraph (1) with any provider of services which is of a type which is required to be certified by the Secretary of Health and Human Services when providing services under title XVIII of the Social Security Act unless the provider is so certified.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: 4 Effective date
children.text: Benefits shall be provided under paragraph (1) on and after .February 1, 1993

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The trustees of the Combined Fund shall provide death benefits coverage to each eligible beneficiary described in subsection (f)(1) which is identical to the benefits provided under the 1950 UMWA Pension Plan or 1974 UMWA Pension Plan, whichever is applicable, on . Such coverage shall be provided on and after .July 20, 1992February 1, 1993

children.heading: Termination of coverage
children.label: 2 Termination of coverage
children.text: The 1950 UMWA Pension Plan and the 1974 UMWA Pension Plan shall each be amended to provide that death benefits coverage shall not be provided to eligible beneficiaries on and after . This paragraph shall not prohibit such plans from subsequently providing death benefits not described in paragraph (1).February 1, 1993

children.heading: Reserves for administration
children.label: d Reserves for administration
children.text: The trustees of the Combined Fund may reserve for each plan year, for use in payment of the administrative costs of the Combined Fund, an amount not to exceed 5 percent of the premiums to be paid to the Combined Fund under section 9704(a) during the plan year.

children.heading: Limitation on enrollment
children.label: e Limitation on enrollment
children.text: The Combined Fund shall not enroll any individual who is not receiving benefits under the 1950 UMWA Benefit Plan or the 1974 UMWA Benefit Plan as of .July 20, 1992
is a coal industry retiree who, on , was eligible to receive, and receiving, benefits from the 1950 UMWA Benefit Plan or the 1974 UMWA Benefit Plan, orJuly 20, 1992
on such date was eligible to receive, and receiving, benefits in either such plan by reason of a relationship to such retiree.
